provenance.source_corpus: Assessing ecological changes in and around marine reserves using community perceptions and biological surveys

source_spans.text: Successful marine protected areas (MPAs), including no-take reserves, can help restore degraded coral reefs and improve the livelihoods of coastal communities by increasing fish size and abundance in adjacent areas
Successful marine protected areas (MPAs), including no-take reserves, can help restore degraded coral reefs
Well-enforced partial or total no-fishing zones (collectively known as marine protected areas, or MPAs) can help restore degraded coral reefs and enhance fish populations.
increasing fish size and abundance in adjacent areas
Handumon MPA was recognized by the MPA Support Network
Results demonstrated that year had a significant positive effect on total fish abundance... inside the reserve
Results demonstrated that year had a significant positive effect on... mid-trophic level fish abundance... inside the reserve
Results demonstrated that year had a significant positive effect on... the total number of families observed inside the reserve
Year had no significant effect on top-trophic fish abundance inside, outside and at reference sites.
There was no significant temporal change in fish sizes for the most frequently observed common large fish families within the reserve

edges.original_sentence: Successful marine protected areas (MPAs), including no-take reserves, can help restore degraded coral reefs and improve the livelihoods of coastal communities by increasing fish size and abundance in adjacent areas (Halpern and Warner, 2002).

source_spans.text: Successful marine protected areas (MPAs), including no-take reserves, can help restore degraded coral reefs and improve the livelihoods of coastal communities by increasing fish size and abundance in adjacent areas (Halpern and Warner, 2002).

source_document.title: Assessing ecological changes in and around marine reserves using community perceptions and biological surveys

edges.original_sentence: Results demonstrated that year had a significant positive effect on total fish abundance, mid-trophic level fish abundance and the total number of families observed inside the reserve, but not immediately outside the reserve (Figure 4, Table 4).
